title: neocheiropteris henryi
intro: Neocheiropteris henryi, also known as Henry's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting liver health, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of Neocheiropteris henryi are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
